name: Fixed Deposit (FD)
suitableFor: Short to medium-term parking of funds
verdict: PPF offers higher post-tax returns due to EEE status. FD is better for short-term needs and flexibility.

name: ELSS Mutual Funds
suitableFor: Higher risk appetite, long-term wealth creation
verdict: ELSS offers potentially higher returns but with market risk. PPF is guaranteed and fully tax-free — ideal for conservative investors.

name: National Pension System (NPS)
taxOnReturns: Partially taxable (60% corpus tax-free, 40% must buy annuity)
risk: Moderate (mix of equity, debt, government securities)
liquidity: Very Low (locked till 60, partial withdrawal after 3 years)
suitableFor: Retirement planning with additional tax benefit
verdict: NPS offers additional ₹50K tax deduction and potentially higher returns. PPF is fully tax-free and more flexible with 15-year lock-in.

name: Sukanya Samriddhi Account (SSA)
suitableFor: Girl child education and marriage planning
verdict: SSA has a higher interest rate and EEE status like PPF, but is only for girl child. PPF is available to everyone with shorter lock-in.

name: Employee Provident Fund (EPF)
taxOnReturns: Tax-free if withdrawn after 5 years of service
liquidity: Low (linked to employment, partial withdrawal for specific purposes)
verdict: EPF has higher rate but is only for salaried employees. PPF is voluntary and available to everyone including self-employed.

name: National Savings Certificate (NSC)
taxOnReturns: Interest taxable, but reinvested interest qualifies for 80C
liquidity: Low (5-year lock-in, no premature withdrawal normally)
suitableFor: Medium-term guaranteed returns with tax benefit
verdict: NSC has shorter lock-in and slightly higher rate, but interest is taxable. PPF's EEE status makes it better for long-term tax-free growth.

name: Recurring Deposit (RD)
suitableFor: Regular monthly savings with guaranteed returns
verdict: RD offers flexibility in tenure but returns are fully taxable and no 80C benefit. PPF is far superior for long-term tax-free wealth building.
